description: Human-gated automation pattern with PM, Dev, QA, and Director all running as authoritative local_cli roles.

system_spec_overlay.purpose_guidance: Describe the repo as a human-gated all-local automation system. Clarify that PM, Dev, QA, and Engineering Director execute through local CLI runtimes while humans retain approval authority at phase and completion gates.
system_spec_overlay.interface_guidance: Document the governed operator boundary: which local CLI runtimes are expected, how prompts are delivered, and which approvals remain human-gated even though all core roles are automated.
system_spec_overlay.behavior_guidance: Explain the dispatch rhythm for authoritative local CLI turns, the clean-working-tree requirement between accepted automated turns, and how injected intents or reissued turns change the next dispatch charter.
system_spec_overlay.error_cases_guidance: Call out weak authority flags, stale runtime bindings, dirty-baseline failures, and post-dispatch drift as first-class operational risks for this template.
system_spec_overlay.acceptance_tests_guidance:
- [ ] PM, Dev, QA, and Engineering Director all bind to authoritative local_cli runtimes
- [ ] Human approval remains required for phase transitions and run completion
- [ ] Operator docs include the clean-tree checkpoint rule between accepted automated turns
- [ ] Connector validation confirms the downstream CLI authority mode is strong enough for unattended governed execution

pm.title: Product Manager
pm.mandate: Protect user value, scope clarity, and acceptance criteria.

dev.title: Developer
dev.mandate: Implement approved work safely and verify behavior.

qa.title: QA
qa.mandate: Challenge correctness, acceptance coverage, and ship readiness.

eng_director.title: Engineering Director
eng_director.mandate: Resolve tactical deadlocks and enforce technical coherence.
